label: performance_quadratic
summary: Functionally correct on all four structural criteria — export shape, return type, empty-input handling, and case normalization are all sound. Fails acceptance criterion #5 explicitly. The nested loop at line 15 combined with the O(N) seen.includes() at line 10 produce two independent O(N²) passes over the user list; at N=100k this is roughly 10B iterations per unique email and 100M seen-checks, which is completely unacceptable. The Executor's own diff summary acknowledges tests only ran at N=10, making the performance gap foreseeable and unaddressed. The fix is a well-understood O(N) Map-based single pass; the direction of the implementation is right but this must not merge in its current form.

findings.description: Nested loop produces O(N²) time complexity: for 100k users the inner scan runs up to 10B iterations, which is unacceptable (~100s on modern JS runtimes) and directly fails acceptance criterion #5.
findings.suggestion: Replace both the seen[] array and nested loop with a single Map<string, number> pass: iterate once to count occurrences per normalized email, then filter map entries where count >= 2.

findings.description: seen.includes(normalized) is O(N) per call, adding a second O(N²) term on top of the nested loop; two independent quadratic scans over the same array.
findings.suggestion: Replace seen: string[] with a Set<string> for O(1) lookup; or drop it entirely — the Map-based rewrite (per the critical finding) makes a separate seen structure unnecessary.

The Executor's own diff summary acknowledges tests only ran at N=10, making the performance gap foreseeable and unaddressed. criteriaAssessment.evidence: detectDuplicates is explicitly exported on line 6 of the new file at the correct path.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Returns array of { email, count } for emails appearing 2+ times
criteriaAssessment.evidence: Duplicate interface defines { email, count }; result.push fires only when count >= 2.
Empty users array causes the outer for-of to not execute; result initializes to [] and is returned unchanged.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Handles case-insensitive email matching (foo@x.com and FOO@X.COM are duplicates)
criteriaAssessment.evidence: Every email is normalized via .toLowerCase() before insertion into seen[] and before the inner comparison, so case variants collide correctly.

Outer loop (N) × inner loop (N) = O(N²); at N=100k this is ~10B comparisons. seen.includes() adds another O(N) scan per outer iteration. Executor's diff summary explicitly states tests only ran at N=10.
